title: Dyable Wools
description: Dyable Wools lets you dye wool directly in crafting tables, making colorful building easier and faster.
body:
# Dyable Wools! 🐑🎨

In **vanilla Minecraft Java Edition**, you cannot dye existing wool blocks in a crafting table—you can only dye white wool or sheep using a crafting recipe or cauldron. **Dyable Wools!** changes that.  

This lightweight datapack adds **shapeless crafting recipes** that let you recolor **any wool block** directly using dyes—no sheep farming or complicated workarounds needed.  

---

## What This Datapack Does

- Craft **any wool color from any other wool color** using **1 wool + 1 dye → 1 wool**  
- Recipes are **shapeless**, **lossless**, and **vanilla-friendly**  
- Works with all existing wool colors  
- **No commands, functions, or client mods** required—fully server-side  

---

## Why Use It?

- Make wool recoloring fast and intuitive  
- Avoid unnecessary sheep farming for different colors  
- Adds a simple, vanilla-style feature inspired by **Minecraft Bedrock Edition**  

---

## Screenshot

![White wool being crafted into red wool](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/241d05339f3eb51e6812d59b597ad63db8b5d573.png)

*Example of recoloring wool using a crafting table*

---

## Compatibility

- Minecraft **Java Edition 1.20 → 1.21.11**  
- Compatible with most other datapacks unless they alter wool dyeing  

---

## Installation

1. Download the `.zip` from the **Versions** tab  
2. Place it in your world or server’s `datapacks` folder  
3. Run `/reload` or restart the server  
4. Recolor wool instantly in a crafting table  

---

## Mod Conversion Information
The mod was converted from a datapack using [https://labs.hardel.io/en-us/converter](https://labs.hardel.io/en-us/converter)
